title: No enforced parity across the library / op-registry / orchestrator-script surfaces — they drift silently
description: The dual-surface principle ('library + tool + script as a unit') is unenforced. The orchestrator scripts build a DispatchContext and forward ctx + optional params (dryRun / ordinal) that the ops drop; the op-registry omits library write fns entirely; nothing catches the divergence at build/test. So projection loss (FGAP-008) and coverage gaps (FGAP-009) accrue silently — e.g. appendToBlock is called WITH ctx by file-block-item.ts:273 and pi-behavior-monitors:1612 but WITHOUT ctx by op append-block-item (ops-registry.ts:174) and pi-workflows step-block.ts:203.

evidence.reference: builds DispatchContext + passes ctx to appendToBlock — op append-block-item drops it
forwards ordinal + ctx to appendRelationByRef — the op forwards ordinal but drops ctx
no test asserts op-registry coverage of the library write surface or ctx/param forwarding parity

impact: FGAP-008 and FGAP-009 recur on every new library writer or op without a guard; the three surfaces silently diverge; attestation correctness and cli completeness can regress undetected.
proposed_resolution: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
